summary: Dominant failure: chapters 10-12 keep resetting and replaying Vel/Kem mistrust on slightly different terrain, so the window reads more episodically than cumulatively on the way to the Scour.

findings.problem: Chapter 10 says the old river crossing was taken out by a flood three years earlier, but chapter 11 frames the same bridge remains as evidence of years of neglect rather than sudden collapse. Read back-to-back, the bridge arrives with two different failure histories.
findings.rewrite_direction: Make the handoff explicit in one direction. Either revise Thessa's route note so the flood finished off an already-rotting bridge, or revise the chapter 11 description so the visible damage still reads as a flood loss compounded by three years of decay.
findings.acceptance_test: A reader moving directly from chapter 10 to chapter 11 can explain the bridge's condition as one coherent chain of cause and effect, without having to choose between flood failure and long neglect.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Later chapter owns the boundary contradiction, and chapter 11 is where the physical description stops matching the route information established in chapter 10.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2143; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2148; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2478; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2483; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2799; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2808
findings.problem: Three consecutive openings convert charged chapter endings into off-page elapsed time. Chapter 10 jumps four days after the threshold reveal, chapter 11 jumps three days after Kem's breach with Vel at Thessa's settlement, and chapter 12 jumps two days past the river lesson. The repeated reset rhythm makes the window feel like a string of set-pieces rather than a compounding run.
findings.rewrite_direction: Break the pattern at a boundary, preferably in chapter 11 or chapter 12. Let one opening begin inside the unresolved aftermath of the prior ending before widening to the next location, so the reader experiences the carryover instead of being told time has passed.
findings.acceptance_test: When chapters 09-12 are read straight through, at least one of the 09/10, 10/11, or 11/12 handoffs now carries immediate emotional or logistical fallout on-page instead of opening with another elapsed-days reorientation.
findings.fix_owner_reason: The repetition becomes a local-window problem because the later handoffs keep choosing the same move; chapter 12 makes it the third consecutive reset opening, so revising the later boundary is the cleanest fix.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2354; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2477; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2671; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2971; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3121; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3123
findings.problem: Across chapters 10-12, the Vel/Kem relationship keeps landing in the same leverage shape: Kem learns or senses that Vel has been steering him toward a Scour-related need, Vel only partially voices the truth, and the chapter closes with the two still walking south under damaged trust. The external triggers change, but the emotional turn keeps re-holding instead of moving to a distinct new state.
findings.rewrite_direction: Make chapter 12 pay off the accumulated pressure instead of replaying it. Either Vel states the budget truth plainly, Kem names the manipulation himself, or the pair explicitly renegotiates the descent on new terms. Do not end on a third version of Vel swallowing the line.
findings.acceptance_test: By the end of chapter 12, the Vel/Kem partnership occupies a materially different state than it did at the end of chapters 10 and 11: explicit terms, explicit breach, or explicit recommitment rather than another silent continuation.
findings.fix_owner_reason: The weaker repetition belongs to the later chapter, and chapter 12 is where the dynamic becomes a third iteration instead of a fresh turn.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2434; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2456; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2458; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2685; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2909; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2941; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2961; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2985; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2993
findings.problem: The local window leans on the same clipped accounting syntax for ability use: a short spend sentence, then an updated total. Because chapters 10-12 already foreground level math as plot, repeating the same tally construction over and over makes the prose start sounding like an expense log instead of escalating dramatic texture.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the arithmetic clear, but vary the delivery. Fold some costs into bodily aftereffects, let some totals arrive a beat later, and reserve the blunt spend-plus-total syntax for only the highest-pressure burns.
findings.acceptance_test: Across chapters 10-12, major spend beats are no longer mostly rendered as a short cost sentence immediately followed by an updated total; the reader can still track the math without hearing the same sentence shape recur in clusters.
findings.fix_owner_reason: This is a cumulative local-window wording pattern, and chapter 12 contains the densest run of identical spend-and-total constructions.
